validationCommand: python -c "import torch; assert hasattr(torch,'xpu') and torch.xpu.is_available(); from src.optimization.memory_manager import is_xpu_available, get_gpu_backend; assert is_xpu_available(); assert get_gpu_backend()=='xpu'; print('SeedVR2 XPU OK')"
knownIssues: memory_manager.py get_device_list() only enumerates CUDA and MPS devices — XPU cards are invisible to the SeedVR2 runtime, so all device selection defaults to CPU or fails.
video_upscaler.py output conversion checks `sample.is_cuda or sample.is_mps` but not `is_xpu`, so tensors on XPU never get moved to CPU for ComfyUI output — silent data loss or crash.
get_basic_vram_info() uses torch.cuda.mem_get_info() with no XPU branch, so VRAM checks return 'No GPU backend available' on Intel systems.
Workflow widgets hardcode `cuda:0` for SeedVR2LoadDiTModel and SeedVR2LoadVAEModel — must be changed to `xpu:0` or `cpu` via runtime policy.
compatibility.py imports FlashAttention/SageAttention unconditionally; these are NVIDIA-only and will crash on import on XPU. Must keep attention as `sdpa`.
Distributed helper basic.py falls back to CUDA/NCCL when MPS is absent — needs an XPU branch or CPU fallback.

workarounds.action: Apply patches/seedvr2-xpu-registration.patch to the SeedVR2 custom node package. The patch adds is_xpu_available() to memory_manager.py, adds XPU to get_device_list() and get_gpu_backend(), adds XPU VRAM query to get_basic_vram_info(), and patches video_upscaler.py output conversion to check getattr(sample, 'is_xpu', False).
workarounds.rationale: These are the minimal changes to make SeedVR2 device-aware on Intel XPU. Without them, the node cannot enumerate XPU devices, query VRAM, or correctly transfer output tensors.
workarounds.tradeoff: The patch adds ~60 lines across 2 files. Must be re-applied if the SeedVR2 custom node package is updated. The patch is against the package's own source (src/), not ComfyUI core.

workarounds.action: Change workflow widget values for SeedVR2LoadDiTModel and SeedVR2LoadVAEModel from `cuda:0` to `xpu:0` (or `cpu` if VRAM is insufficient). Record this as a runtime-policy change.
workarounds.rationale: The workflow JSON hardcodes CUDA device selection. Even with the patch, the device must be explicitly set to XPU.
workarounds.tradeoff: Requires a deliberate workflow modification — do not silently edit. Must be recorded as a migration decision.

workarounds.action: If VRAM headroom is insufficient for full-resolution upscaling (SeedVR2 target can be 3840×7680), run a reduced-scope branch smoke first (e.g. 256×256 input), then scale up.
workarounds.rationale: SeedVR2 DiT intermediate tensors can be 4-8× the input resolution. On XPU with <16GB VRAM, full-resolution will OOM.
workarounds.tradeoff: Slower validation cycle; full-resolution run deferred until memory math justifies it.

retireCondition: SeedVR2 custom node package adds native XPU device enumeration, VRAM query, and output tensor conversion upstream. Track ComfyUI-SeedVR2_VideoUpscaler releases.
